exemplar.title: How has Netflixâs decision to remove its Basic Plan without ads and reintroduce a Basic Plan with ads impacted customer satisfaction and subscription rates?

detailedExemplarData.title: How has Netflixâs decision to remove its Basic Plan without ads and reintroduce a Basic Plan with ads impacted customer satisfaction and subscription rates?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Consistent framing of the investigation around the concept of change throughout the introduction, analysis and conclusion
Clear references to the âcomplexity of changeâ in the force-field table and ratio analysis
Strong, Netflix-specific examples (subscriber numbers, price points, catalogue gaps) anchoring the concept
weaknesses: Not all analytical paragraphs explicitly tie back to the key concept of change
Background/history section describes facts without linking them to change drivers
Occasional generic statements about streaming services dilute organizational focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work in Band 4: the student shows good understanding and clear analytical thinking around 'change' and makes a strong organizational connection. Integration is high-quality but not fully seamless or deeply threaded in every section, meriting 4/5.

strengths: Four supporting documents provided, meeting the 3â5 requirement
Documents cover quantitative (financials, subscriber data) and qualitative (industry articles) perspectives
Appendix 1âs force-field justification demonstrates depth of analysis
weaknesses: Reliance on Reddit as evidence raises concerns about reliability
Timeline document could better link events to subscription data points
Depth could be enhanced by tying Variety article insights back to customer satisfaction metrics
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While relevance and depth are generally good (Band 4), limitations in source reliability and opportunities to deepen links to the research question place this at Band 3 (3/4).

strengths: Selection of Porterâs Five Forces, force-field analysis and ratio analysis is appropriate for the research question
Tools are applied correctly with generally clear relevance to Netflixâs strategy
Ratio calculations and force-field scores are presented accurately
weaknesses: Justification for choosing these specific tools is brief
Some labels (e.g. Porter axes) are ambiguous without narrative clarification
Discussion of competitors is superficial and could benefit from a comparative matrix
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments rate selection and application as adequate (Band 3). The tools are appropriate and correctly used, but justification and depth of application are not consistently convincing, warranting 3/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Adequate use of three-year financial data in tables and discussion
Some analysis links changes in gross profit margins to strategic decisions
Appendix 2 shows thorough step-by-step ratio calculations
weaknesses: Lacks direct customer-satisfaction metrics or triangulating data
Analysis is patchy, with contradictions between sections
Causal links (e.g., between price changes and subscriber trends) are asserted without strong evidence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis and evaluation fall into Band 3: there is clear use of data and some analytical thinking, but integration across sections is uneven and depth of evaluation is moderate, justifying 3/5.

strengths: Conclusions summarize both positive and negative impacts
References earlier evidence from analysis to support points
weaknesses: Internal contradictions undermine clarity (e.g., stating satisfaction rose but calling impact inconclusive)
Final stance is âinconclusiveâ without an explicit, reasoned judgment addressing the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place conclusions in Band 2: they are partially consistent with evidence and only partially answer the research question, meriting 2/3.

strengths: Clear table of contents and logical headings facilitate navigation
Appendices and candidate details are appropriately placed
Structure throughout the IA is coherent and appropriate
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA demonstrates an appropriate, well-organised structure throughout (Band 2), so it earns full marks in this criterion.

strengths: All major required elements are present (title page, word count, bibliography, appendices)
weaknesses: Bibliography shows inconsistent formatting and missing access dates
In-text citation style is not uniformly applied across the document
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the necessary elements are included, multiple presentation issues (incomplete code, inconsistent citations, missing access dates) prevent full compliance, placing this at Band 1 (1/2).

strandSelections.reasoning: A clear table of contents, logical headings and correctly placed appendices demonstrate an appropriate overall structure for an IA.

Each document is tightly linked to the research question (pricing article, Nasdaq financials, CNN subscriber data, Variety content-gap piece).  Together they provide quantitative, qualitative and industry context, giving good depth and a reasonable range of perspectives.
The title page, word count, candidate/session details, bibliography, and appendices are all present, meeting IB presentation requirements.
Four distinct supporting documents are appended, keeping the total within the 3-5 range specified for the higher bands.

Tools are applied with reasonable accuracy: Porter identifies cost leadership, the force-field is scored, and ratios are calculated correctly.  Links to customer satisfaction are occasionally asserted rather than evidenced, so relevance is adequate but not consistently convincing.

The IA draws on three-year financial data and qualitative findings; figures are used in tables and discussion, showing adequate selection and application.  Absence of direct customer-satisfaction metrics and limited triangulation keep it from the higher bands.

Analysis explains movements in margins and links force-field scores to decisions, demonstrating some analytical thinking.  Yet integration across sections is patchy and conclusions sometimes contradict earlier statements, so overall quality is moderate rather than mostly effective.
The student ultimately labels the impact âinconclusiveâ, so the research question is only partially answered; an explicit, reasoned judgement is missing.

Porter, Force-Field and Ratio analysis represent an adequate and conventional tool-set for this type of IA.  While appropriate, the selection is not especially wide or innovative, so it sits comfortably in the âadequateâ band rather than âeffectiveâ.

Conclusions acknowledge both positive and negative impacts and reference earlier evidence, but internal contradictions (e.g., stating customer satisfaction rose then labelling impact âinconclusiveâ) show only partial consistency.

Most arguments are anchored in Netflix-specific evidence: subscriber numbers, price points, missing catalogue titles and three-year profitability ratios.  Tools are applied directly to the firm, and supporting documents all relate to Netflix.  Minor sections lapse into generic statements about âstreaming servicesâ, so the organisational link is very strong but not wholly integrated everywhere.

The student consistently frames the investigation around the concept of change, linking Netflixâs plan alterations to shifting competitive conditions and customer expectations.  Clear references to the âcomplexity of changeâ appear in the introduction, analysis, force-field table and conclusion, demonstrating a good conceptual grasp.  However, the concept is not threaded through every analytical paragraphâdiscussion often reverts to cost or content advantages without explicitly tying these back to changeâso the integration is strong but not truly seamless.

annotations.comment: The key concept âChangeâ is correctly identified, but the candidate code field is incomplete. Ensure the candidate code is clearly stated and formatted according to IB requirements.
The research question is concise and focused, clearly defining the impact of Netflixâs plan changes on satisfaction and subscriptions.
The table of contents demonstrates a clear, logical structure, aiding navigation and reflecting good organization.
The background section provides descriptive history but misses linking these facts explicitly back to the concept of change. Tie each point to the evolution drivers.
The justification for tool selection is brief. Expand on why Porterâs, force-field and ratio analyses are the most appropriate to answer the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The finding about unsubscribing users is asserted without quantitative data or citation detail. Include subscriber numbers or percentages to support this claim.
The discussion of missing titles notes 10%, but lacks depth on customer impact. Consider adding survey or social-media sentiment data to quantify dissatisfaction.
Porterâs matrix is neatly presented but the axes labels are ambiguous. Label 'Market Scope' and 'Competitive Advantage' explicitly in narrative to clarify application.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The reliance on Reddit as evidence is problematic due to variable reliability. Supplement with industry or academic studies to strengthen qualitative support.
The claim of higher customer satisfaction lacks measurable metrics. Integrate survey scores or Net Promoter Score data to substantiate satisfaction changes.
The comparative discussion of competitor features is superficial. Develop a competitor matrix to differentiate Netflixâs offerings versus peers.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The force-field table sums to driving 19 and restraining 14, but no main-text justification is provided here (only in appendix). Summarise key rationales in the body.
Attributing Netflixâs decision chiefly to pricing needs more causal evidence. Link subscription growth trendlines directly to price changes.
The alignment with industry trends is noted, but lacks supporting data such as competitor adoption rates or industry reports.
The ethical concern of over-targeted ads is raised but is not explicitly tied back to the key concept of âchangeâ. Link the ethical implications to how change affects stakeholder trust.
The gross profit margin table is clear but omits industry benchmarks. Include average sector margins to contextualize Netflixâs performance.
The narrative around GPM changes lacks critical evaluation of external factors (e.g., COVID-19) versus plan changes. Distinguish these influences.
Introducing the net profit margin section with only a heading misses a brief definition or formula. Add the formula here for clarity.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The cost and marketing cost discussion lacks consistent citation style; include full reference details and page or paragraph numbers.
The conclusion opening quote is engaging but lacks an in-text citation format. Provide proper IB citation style for the Perdue quote.
The conclusion effectively summarizes methods but fails to deliver a clear, reasoned judgement. Strengthen the ending with an explicit answer to the research question.
The bibliography contains varied formats and missing access dates. Adopt a consistent citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) across all entries.
Several sources lack access dates. Ensure every online reference includes an access date for completeness.
Appendix 1 provides thorough force-field justification, demonstrating a good depth of analysis.
Appendix 2 accurately presents the gross profit margin formula in LaTeX and shows step-by-step calculations.
The supporting documents section clearly labels each document and links content to the research question.
The Netflix price-hikes timeline is useful, but the link to the RQ timeframe could be made explicit by correlating events to subscription data points.
The comprehensive Nasdaq financial table is well structured but could be streamlined by focusing on the most relevant figures for ratio analysis.
The CNN article offering subscriber numbers is very relevant and adds strong industry context to subscription trends.
The Variety article on missing titles enriches understanding of content limitations but could be tied back to customer satisfaction data for deeper analysis.
